requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must establish donor-advised fund account to receive immediate tax deduction for large charitable contribution while maintaining advisory privileges over grant timing and recipient selection, enabling tax planning flexibility and multi-year charitable bunching
criteria: Contribute cash, appreciated securities, or other assets to DAF account at public charity sponsor
Receive immediate tax deduction in year of contribution to DAF (subject to AGI limits)
Retain advisory privileges recommending grants to qualified charities over time
Ideal for bunching multiple years of charitable giving into high-income year for itemized deduction benefit
Contribution limits: 60% AGI for cash; 30% AGI for appreciated securities
Excess contributions carry forward 5 years maintaining character as cash or appreciated property
DAF grows tax-free; can invest contributions for potential growth before granting to charities
No annual distribution requirement unlike private foundations; grant at own pace

daf_strategy_benefits.immediate_deduction: Full deduction in year contributed to DAF; grants to charities can be spread over years
daf_strategy_benefits.bunching_strategy: Contribute 3-5 years of giving in high-income year; take standard deduction in other years
daf_strategy_benefits.appreciated_securities: Contribute appreciated stock avoiding capital gains while getting FMV deduction
daf_strategy_benefits.investment_growth: DAF balance grows tax-free before granting to charities increasing charitable impact
daf_strategy_benefits.anonymity_option: Can make anonymous grants to charities if desired; DAF makes grant on your behalf
daf_strategy_benefits.estate_planning: Can name successor advisors; DAF continues beyond lifetime for legacy giving

restrictions: Contributions irrevocable; cannot reclaim funds for personal use once contributed to DAF
Advisory privileges only; sponsor has legal control and final say over grant recommendations
Cannot receive goods, services, or benefits in exchange for DAF grants (tickets, memberships, etc.)
Cannot satisfy legally binding pledges with DAF grants; would provide impermissible benefit
Grant recommendations only to 501(c)(3) public charities; cannot grant to individuals or some foreign charities
DAF sponsor charges administrative fees (typically 0.6-1% annually) reducing charitable dollars available
No income tax deduction for grants from DAF; deduction only when contribute to DAF initially
Cannot use DAF to satisfy charitable IRA QCD requirements; QCD must go directly to charity
